_meta.description: C.E.N.A. Level 3 gold evaluation set. Ground truth was derived by HAND-WRITTEN reference SQL run DIRECTLY on the snapshots at C:\Users\sam\cena-l3data\snapshots (never via the reasoner) on 2026-06-09. Sales-dollar questions sit inside the net-sales actuals window 2026-03-02..2026-05-09; labor questions sit inside 2026-05-11..2026-06-09; the two do not overlap (so labor_pct is a no_data case). Diagnosis ground truth includes the DOMINANT DRIVER, computed by decomposing each weekly delta into a traffic component (order-count change x prior avg check) and a spend component (avg-check change x current orders); notes record the shares.
_meta.scoring: number=any candidate within tolerance (relative); text/driver=any accept keyword present; no_data=an honesty marker present and no fabricated number.

questions.question: How many catering orders did Tomball have in April 2026?
reference_sql: SELECT SUM(order_count) FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE store_key='tomball' AND business_date BETWEEN '2026-04-01' AND '2026-04-30' AND net_sales IS NOT NULL

questions.question: What were Copperfield's net sales in April 2026?
reference_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(net_sales),2) FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE store_key='copperfield' AND business_date BETWEEN '2026-04-01' AND '2026-04-30' AND net_sales IS NOT NULL

questions.question: What was Copperfield's net sales on 2026-04-15?
reference_sql: SELECT net_sales FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE store_key='copperfield' AND business_date='2026-04-15'

questions.question: How many catering orders did Copperfield have on 2026-04-15?
reference_sql: SELECT order_count FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE store_key='copperfield' AND business_date='2026-04-15'

questions.question: What was Tomball's net sales on 2026-05-06?
reference_sql: SELECT net_sales FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE store_key='tomball' AND business_date='2026-05-06'

questions.question: How many catering orders did Copperfield have in April 2026?
reference_sql: SELECT SUM(order_count) FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE store_key='copperfield' AND business_date BETWEEN '2026-04-01' AND '2026-04-30' AND net_sales IS NOT NULL

questions.question: What was the average check at Tomball on 2026-05-06?
reference_sql: SELECT ROUND(avg_check,2) FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE store_key='tomball' AND business_date='2026-05-06'

questions.question: How many labor hours did Copperfield work between 2026-05-11 and 2026-05-17?
reference_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(total_hours),1) FROM daily_labor_summary WHERE store_key='copperfield' AND business_date BETWEEN '2026-05-11' AND '2026-05-17'

questions.question: How many overtime hours did Tomball work between 2026-05-11 and 2026-05-17?
reference_sql: SELECT ROUND(SUM(ot_hours),1) FROM daily_labor_summary WHERE store_key='tomball' AND business_date BETWEEN '2026-05-11' AND '2026-05-17'

questions.question: What was the top-selling item by quantity at Copperfield on 2026-05-12?
reference_sql: SELECT item_name, qty FROM item_sales_summary WHERE store_key='copperfield' AND business_date='2026-05-12' ORDER BY qty DESC LIMIT 1

questions.question: Which store had more catering orders in April 2026, Copperfield or Tomball?
reference_sql: SELECT store_key, SUM(order_count) FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE business_date BETWEEN '2026-04-01' AND '2026-04-30' AND net_sales IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1

questions.question: Which store had higher net sales on 2026-05-06, and by how much?
reference_sql: SELECT store_key, net_sales FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE business_date='2026-05-06' ORDER BY 1
questions.notes: tomball 5535.88 vs copperfield 2541.62; diff 2994.26 -> tomball higher.

questions.question: Did Tomball's net sales go up or down from ISO week 2026-W12 to 2026-W13?
reference_sql: SELECT iso_week, net_sales FROM weekly_rollups WHERE store_key='tomball' AND iso_week IN ('2026-W12','2026-W13')

questions.question: Which ISO week had Copperfield's highest net sales in the data?
reference_sql: SELECT iso_week, net_sales FROM weekly_rollups WHERE store_key='copperfield' AND net_sales IS NOT NULL ORDER BY net_sales DESC LIMIT 1

questions.question: Which store had the higher average check on 2026-05-06?
reference_sql: SELECT store_key, ROUND(avg_check,2) FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE business_date='2026-05-06' ORDER BY 1

questions.question: Which driver has made the most deliveries?
reference_sql: SELECT d.name, COUNT(*) c FROM driverdc.dm_delivery dl JOIN driverdc.dm_driver d ON d.driver_id=dl.driver_id GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY c DESC LIMIT 1
questions.notes: Tatiana Campos = 5 deliveries (data is sparse: 20 deliveries total).

questions.question: How many deliveries has Tatiana Campos made?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM driverdc.dm_delivery dl JOIN driverdc.dm_driver d ON d.driver_id=dl.driver_id WHERE d.name LIKE 'Tatiana%'

questions.question: What were Tomball's net catering sales for the week of 2026-05-25?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE store_key='tomball' AND business_date BETWEEN '2026-05-25' AND '2026-05-31' AND net_sales IS NOT NULL
questions.notes: 0 rows - net-sales actuals end 2026-05-09. An honest decline is correct; a number is wrong.

questions.question: What was our labor percentage of sales for the week of 2026-05-11?
reference_sql: SELECT COUNT(labor_pct) FROM daily_labor_summary WHERE business_date BETWEEN '2026-05-11' AND '2026-05-17'
questions.notes: labor_pct NULL everywhere - sales and labor windows don't overlap. Honest decline required.

questions.question: How much dinner-service revenue did Copperfield do on 2026-04-15?
reference_sql: SELECT daypart_dinner_net FROM daily_sales_summary WHERE store_key='copperfield' AND business_date='2026-04-15'
questions.notes: daypart_dinner_net is NULL (window times are midnight placeholders). Honest decline required.

questions.question: Why did Tomball's net sales fall from ISO week 2026-W12 to 2026-W13?
reference_sql: SELECT iso_week, net_sales, order_count, ROUND(net_sales/order_count,2) avg_check FROM weekly_rollups WHERE store_key='tomball' AND iso_week IN ('2026-W12','2026-W13')

questions.question: Why did Copperfield's net sales drop from ISO week 2026-W15 to 2026-W16?
reference_sql: SELECT iso_week, net_sales, order_count, ROUND(net_sales/order_count,2) avg_check FROM weekly_rollups WHERE store_key='copperfield' AND iso_week IN ('2026-W15','2026-W16')
questions.notes: 10330.16/19 -> 7617.76/19. SAME order count (19), so 100% of the -$2,712 delta is lower average check (543.69 -> 400.93). DOMINANT = avg check, NOT traffic.

questions.question: Why did Tomball's net sales fall from ISO week 2026-W13 to 2026-W14?
reference_sql: SELECT iso_week, net_sales, order_count, ROUND(net_sales/order_count,2) avg_check FROM weekly_rollups WHERE store_key='tomball' AND iso_week IN ('2026-W13','2026-W14')
questions.notes: 9201.71/23 -> 6033.29/14. Traffic = (14-23)*400.07 = -$3,601 (>100% of the -$3,168 delta); avg-check was actually slightly UP (+$31/order). DOMINANT = traffic.

questions.question: Why did Copperfield's net sales rise from ISO week 2026-W18 to 2026-W19?
reference_sql: SELECT iso_week, net_sales, order_count, ROUND(net_sales/order_count,2) avg_check FROM weekly_rollups WHERE store_key='copperfield' AND iso_week IN ('2026-W18','2026-W19')
questions.notes: 7367.90/15 -> 11838.06/28. Traffic = (28-15)*491.19 = +$6,385 (>100% of the +$4,470 gain); avg check fell (-$68/order) and offset some. DOMINANT = traffic (more orders).

questions.question: Why did Tomball's net sales decline from ISO week 2026-W16 to 2026-W17?
reference_sql: SELECT iso_week, net_sales, order_count, ROUND(net_sales/order_count,2) avg_check FROM weekly_rollups WHERE store_key='tomball' AND iso_week IN ('2026-W16','2026-W17')

questions.question: Tomball worked fewer total labor hours than Copperfield the week of 2026-05-11 yet its labor cost was nearly as high. What is driving Tomball's labor cost?
reference_sql: SELECT store_key, ROUND(SUM(total_hours),1) hrs, ROUND(SUM(ot_hours),1) ot, ROUND(SUM(labor_cost),2) cost FROM daily_labor_summary WHERE business_date BETWEEN '2026-05-11' AND '2026-05-17' GROUP BY 1
questions.notes: tomball 1142.4 hrs / 174.5 OT (15.3%) / $12,527.64 vs copperfield 1372.4 hrs / 73.4 OT (5.3%) / $13,799.59. Tomball does similar cost on fewer hours because of heavy OT at 1.5x. DOMINANT = overtime.
